_comment: DE440 state vectors at 27 annual epochs (J2000.0 through J2026.0) for all Sol celestial bodies. J2026.0 is the project epoch (matches sol_complex.json system_time). Annual snapshots enable cubic Hermite interpolation for continuous local prediction. J2000.0 and all intermediate epochs serve as validation targets.

reference_frame.description: International Celestial Reference Frame, equatorial coordinates

sources.planetary_gm: JPL Horizons body parameters (DE440, Park et al. 2021)
sources.satellite_gm: JPL Satellite Physical Parameters (JUP365, SAT441, URA111, NEP095, PLU058)

notes.mass_precision: All masses derived as GM x 10^9 / G; inherit G's ~2.2e-5 relative uncertainty
notes.gm_precision: GM values are directly measured from ephemeris fits, known to far higher precision than mass
notes.state_vector_precision: Inner planets: sub-km; outer planets: ~1-10 km; Pluto: ~tens of km
notes.test_strategy: Four-way comparison: Our Model (DE440s + bary offsets), Numpy N-body (leapfrog from J2026.0), Local Hermite interpolation (from annual snapshots), all compared against JPL Horizons ground truth.
notes.annual_snapshots: 27 annual epochs from J2000.0 to J2026.0, each containing SSB-centered ICRF state vectors (position + velocity) for all 26 bodies. Enables cubic Hermite interpolation between epochs for continuous local prediction at arbitrary times.

bary_to_center_offsets._description: Offset vectors from planetary system barycenter to planet center of mass, computed from JPL Horizons (barycenter code N vs center code N99). DE440s only provides barycenter segments for outer planets; these offsets allow correction to planet-center positions.
